segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They slipped out at first light, when Millbrook's smoke was still thin and blue and the streets were quiet except for scavenging crows.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The palisade gate stood propped with a beam Thornik had reset the night before -- his last job in the town -- and it creaked in the morning chill, wood resin sharp in the air.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The farewells were already done. They'd happened at a mile-stone a mile back, where the road forked three ways and people they trusted had taken two of the other turnings. What remained was just four shadows crossing frost-stiff grass, heading northeast.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin set an easy pace. The Ember's coal-glow tucked beneath a fresh wrap. The ribbon Nyxara had knotted at his wrist lay soft and cool against skin, its silver thread no darker than yesterday. He didn't tug at it. He resisted the urge to.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Your stride's heavier,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said eventually, mismatched eyes flicking to his arm and away again. She was moving carefully herself -- her ribs logged every twist of the torso, and she'd learned to stop twisting.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Hurts more in the cold,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin admitted.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "It'll level out."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan walked rear guard, winter-pale and deliberate. He scanned the hedgerows and the ditch lines, not the road. When he stepped, his shadow stepped with him -- with an effort most people wouldn't have noticed, but Vex did. She pretended not to.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric walked between Durgan and the rest, which seemed to be where he'd decided he fit. He carried his medical bag slung across one shoulder and moved with the unhurried economy of a man who'd learned to conserve what he had a long time ago.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He hadn't said anything since the mile-stone bend. He didn't appear to need to.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The first hour taught Caelin the new rhythm. It wasn't worse than the old one -- just different. Quieter. No Thornik clicking and whirring, filling the silence with observations about geology and family anecdotes.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: No Serana's armored tread, steady as a drum. No Nyxara's dry commentary, arriving at exactly the moment it was least expected and most needed. No Elowen's calm, the silence of someone who listened to the forest and let the forest listen back.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Four was leaner. Four was faster. Four was also lonelier, and lonelier was its own kind of weight.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: By the time the road bent past the last shepherd's croft, the smells had changed from dung smoke and malt to iron-rich streamwater and thawed loam. A farmer had nailed a square of parchment to a boundary post. Wind worried its corners.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex plucked it free, skimmed, snorted.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "You're famous."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin didn't need the illustration to know. The crude sketch got the angles of his face wrong but captured the impossible: a black mark in a man's forearm.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Alive,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex read.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "One thousand gold. More for the mark intact."

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Price is climbing,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan said, voice flat.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Means we're interesting,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex replied.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Hate that."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric leaned over and read the poster without taking it from Vex.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "The illustration's generous. You've got better cheekbones than that."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He walked on.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Also a thousand gold is insultingly low for whatever that thing in your arm actually is. Someone's being stingy."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They left the sign flapping on its nail. Crows watched from a hedge, heads cocked, the way crows watch everything -- with the patient assessment of creatures that know death is coming and are simply interested in the schedule.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Two miles on, at a wayside shrine toppled by frost heave, Caelin dropped to a knee to tighten his boot. When he cinched the lace and reached for his pack, his fingers brushed paper that hadn't been there when they left town.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He froze. Then palmed it out.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Cream stock. No seal. Three words in an elegant hand that shifted colors in the light like oil on water:

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: North by iron. Two days' grace. -- L

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A prismatic dusting ran across the letters and vanished.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex caught the flick of paper.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Problem?"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Map note,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, folding it into his sleeve. Not a lie. Not the truth.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She eyed him a beat longer than was comfortable, then nodded once.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Good. We could use a map."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They rose and walked.
